subject: 1623 50th Ave - weeds, junk, nobody living there
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I live two doors down at 1619 50th Ave and I am writing about the house at 1623 50th Ave, between Madison and Monroe. It has been empty since around October last year. The grass and weeds are easily knee-high, well past the 8-inch limit I know the village allows, and there is a pile of busted furniture, a torn mattress, and three or four trash bags rotting on the side parkway. Last week somebody dumped an old box TV and what looks like car parts in the back driveway. I called the village hotline twice in March and once already this month and nothing has changed. The gutters are hanging off the front and one is laying in the bushes. We keep our block nice on this stretch - the Pawlaks, the folks at 1631, all of us mow and shovel - and this one property is dragging the whole street down and I worry about my home value. Can someone from code enforcement please actually come out and look? I have photos from April 3, April 9, and April 14 showing it getting worse and I am happy to send them. I just want somebody to make the owner clean it up or have the village do it. Thank you for your time.

subject: 1623 50th Ave complaint - please open a case
body_clean: Sandra, forwarding a resident complaint from Mr. Halloran at 1619 50th Ave about the property at 1623 50th Ave. He reports tall weeds well over our 8-inch maximum, illegally dumped furniture, a mattress, trash bags on the parkway, and now auto parts and a TV in the rear driveway. He says he called the hotline three times since March with no response, which concerns me. Please open a formal code case, do a site inspection this week, and let me know what we are dealing with - is this an occupied home, a rental, or truly abandoned? If the owner is out of area we should pull the deed and tax records through Cook County and the clerk's office so we have a verified mailing address before we send anything. Please copy Karen and Yolanda so the village manager and clerk are looped in from the start, and give me a one-line status by Friday. I told Mr. Halloran he would hear back, so let us make sure that happens. Thank you.

subject: Inspection results 1623 50th Ave - case CE-2025-0418
body_clean: Mayor, Karen, Yolanda - I inspected 1623 50th Ave on April 17 and opened case CE-2025-0418. Findings confirm Mr. Halloran's report and then some. Weeds and grass measured 14 to 22 inches across the front and side yards, violating Village Code Sec. 30-12 (weeds/rank vegetation, 8-inch max). Accumulated debris on the parkway and rear drive - mattress, three furniture pieces, roughly a dozen trash bags, a CRT television and assorted auto parts - violates Sec. 30-37 (accumulation of refuse and junk). The rear service door is unsecured and standing open, and a first-floor window on the south side is broken out, which puts it in violation of Sec. 14-211 (securing of vacant buildings). The property appears genuinely vacant; mail is overflowing and there is no utility activity per a quick check. Per the clerk's deed pull the owner of record is Raymond T. Voss, mailing address in Mesa, Arizona - an out-of-state landlord, not owner-occupied. I posted a notice on the door April 17 and will send the formal Notice of Violation by certified mail to the Mesa address today, giving 15 days to abate the weeds and refuse and to secure the structure, with daily fines of $100 per violation per day accruing after the deadline under Sec. 1-9. I have referred the open structure and possible rodent/sanitation issues to Priya. Photos are in the case file.

subject: Rats coming from 1623 50th Ave into my yard
body_clean: Dear Mayor, I am the neighbor directly behind the empty house, my property at 1620 Monroe St backs up to the rear lot of 1623 50th Ave. I am writing because we now have a serious rat problem and I am certain it is coming from that abandoned house. Over the last three weeks I have seen rats in broad daylight - at least five or six different times - running along my fence line and into the trash and that rotting mattress piled in their back driveway. My grandkids play in our yard and I will not let them out back anymore. I found burrow holes along the foundation on the alley side of 1623 and droppings all over my garbage cans. I have lived on Monroe for 22 years and never seen anything like this. I have already spent about $140 on traps and bait of my own and it is not enough because the source is next door and nobody is doing anything about it. Whoever owns that place left garbage and an open building to rot all winter. Please send the health department out. I am told the village can do rodent abatement and bill the owner. I would really appreciate someone calling me back - this is a health issue with children in three of the houses on this block.

subject: Rodent abatement ordered 1623 50th Ave - case CE-2025-0418
body_clean: Mayor, Sandra, Karen - following the rat complaint from Ms. Okafor-Brown at 1620 Monroe St, I inspected the rear and alley side of 1623 50th Ave on May 6. I confirmed an active rodent harborage: at least four open burrows along the alley-side foundation, fresh droppings and gnaw marks at the refuse pile and mattress, and runways through the overgrowth. This is a clear violation of Sec. 30-65 (rodent harborage and infestation) and a public health nuisance. I have issued a rodent abatement order to the owner of record, Raymond T. Voss in Mesa, AZ, layered on top of Sandra's existing Notice of Violation, requiring professional extermination and removal of the harborage material within 10 days. Given children on the block and rats active in daylight - which indicates a heavy population - I do not recommend waiting the full owner-response window. I recommend the village authorize our contract exterminator to bait the burrows and alley now under our nuisance-abatement authority and bill it back to the owner with the lien. Estimated cost for the initial knockdown baiting is $480 to $650. I have advised Ms. Okafor-Brown to stop her own baiting near where children play and given her the contractor's name. Please confirm I can dispatch our contractor this week.

subject: Authorizing emergency rodent abatement and tracking costs 1623 50th Ave
body_clean: Priya, yes - you are authorized to dispatch our contract exterminator this week for the initial knockdown baiting at 1623 50th Ave, not to exceed $650 from the nuisance abatement line. Public health with children on the block clears my bar for acting ahead of the owner-response window under our nuisance authority. Please document everything for cost recovery. Sandra, I want a single running ledger on case CE-2025-0418 so we can attach every dollar to the eventual lien: certified mailing costs, the daily fines accruing under Sec. 1-9, the exterminator invoice, and whatever weed-cutting and debris hauling Public Works ends up doing. David in Finance should be looped so the charges post correctly and we can certify the lien amount cleanly later. As of today the owner Mr. Voss has not responded to the April 17 Notice of Violation. If the 15-day abatement window has lapsed with no contact, start the daily fines clock and confirm the date the fines begin. I also want Frank's crew to price out cutting the weeds and hauling the refuse so we are ready to do the work ourselves and bill it. Let us be methodical here - out-of-state absentee owners often only respond once real money and a lien are on the table.

subject: your notices on 50th Ave property
body_clean: To whom it concerns at Bellwood village hall, I received some certified letters about the property on 50th Ave with a lot of fines on them. I want to say up front this is not really my problem to deal with right now. I am out in Arizona and I have not been able to get back there. I had a tenant who trashed the place and left, and frankly the house is more trouble than it is worth. I am not paying $100 a day in fines that I did not even know were adding up - I travel and do not always check that mailbox. As far as the weeds, I cannot exactly fly out to mow a lawn in Illinois. If the village wants to cut it go ahead, but I am not going to be on the hook for some big bill. I may just let it go to the bank or sell it as-is to whoever will take it. Tell me what the actual minimum is to make these letters stop. I am one person, not a big company, and I think the daily fine business is excessive for an empty house that is not hurting anybody. Send me one number and I will think about it.

subject: Response to owner and status update CE-2025-0418
body_clean: Mayor, Karen, Yolanda - Mr. Voss finally responded, attached above, and it is exactly the evasive non-answer we expected. He acknowledges receiving the notices, declines to abate, and is fishing for a discount while floating walking away from the property. To set the record straight on the numbers: the April 17 Notice of Violation gave 15 days; the abatement window lapsed May 2 with no contact, so daily fines under Sec. 1-9 began accruing May 3. As of June 16 that is 45 days at $100/day per violation across three live violations (weeds Sec. 30-12, refuse Sec. 30-37, unsecured structure Sec. 14-211), though I am applying enforcement discretion and counting it conservatively. The running ledger now stands at roughly $6,200 in fines plus $592 for the exterminator, $410 for the board-up of the open door and south window that PW completed June 5, and pending weed-cut and haul charges. I will send Mr. Voss a formal reply stating there is no negotiated minimum - he must abate or reimburse the village's abatement costs, and the accrued fines stand unless adjudicated. I do not have authority to settle the fines; that is a legal/adjudication question. Recommend we refer to the clerk and village attorney now and that Rosa do an unsafe-structure evaluation given the open building and his stated intent to abandon it.

subject: Unsafe structure determination 1623 50th Ave
body_clean: Mayor, Karen, Sandra, Yolanda - I completed a structural evaluation of 1623 50th Ave on July 9 with Tom Reyes assisting on the exterior assessment, and I am issuing a formal unsafe-structure determination under Sec. 14-205 (Dangerous and Unsafe Buildings). Conditions: the rear roof over the kitchen addition has partially collapsed with active water intrusion and visible sagging of the ceiling joists; the south foundation wall shows a stepped crack roughly a quarter-inch wide running about six feet; the front porch deck is rotted through and the railing is gone; and despite the June 5 board-up, the south window has been pried open again and there is clear evidence of unauthorized entry - bedding, food wrappers, and burn marks on the floor near the rear bedroom where someone has had open flame inside a wood-frame house with no utilities. That last item is the most urgent: this is an active fire hazard and a life-safety risk to anyone entering and to the attached-grade neighbors. I am declaring the structure unsafe and unfit for occupancy and posting a placard. Options now are repair to code under permit, or demolition. Given the collapsed roof, the squatter activity, and the absentee owner who has stated he intends to abandon it, my professional recommendation is to move toward the demolition track under our dangerous-buildings process while the legal/lien process runs in parallel. I recommend a re-board and securing of the site this week, and notifying the fire department of the hazard.

subject: People living in the abandoned house - fire risk
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, following up on my April complaint about 1623 50th Ave. The weeds did get cut at some point, thank you, but the situation is now much worse and frankly scary. There are people getting into that house at night. My wife and I have seen flashlights moving inside after dark at least four times in the last two weeks, and twice we have smelled smoke and seen what looked like a small fire glow through the broken south window. There is no power to that house so whatever they are burning is an open fire inside a wooden building that is twelve feet from the Pawlaks' garage and not much further from our house. The village boarded it up in the spring but the boards on that side window are off again. I called the police non-emergency line two nights ago and an officer came through but the house was quiet by then. I am genuinely afraid this place is going to burn and take a neighbor's home with it, or someone is going to die inside. We have small kids on this block. I do not know what the legal hold-up is with the owner but I am begging the village to either secure this building for real or tear it down before someone gets hurt. Please.

subject: Lien certification and village attorney referral 1623 50th Ave
body_clean: Mayor, Karen - here is the legal status on 1623 50th Ave, case CE-2025-0418, consolidating everyone's work. I have referred the matter to the village attorney, Patterson & Cole, who opened file BW-2025-117. The certified-cost ledger David and I reconciled as of September 15 totals $14,847.20: accrued daily fines of $11,900 (computed conservatively per Sandra's enforcement discretion), exterminator and follow-up baiting $1,065, the June board-up and July re-securing by Public Works $1,180, two weed-cuts and debris hauling $702.20. I am preparing to certify these as a municipal lien against the parcel under Sec. 1-9 and Sec. 14-216, recorded with the Cook County Recorder, so the village recovers its costs at any future sale or transfer. On the building itself, the attorney advises we proceed under the Illinois dangerous-buildings statute: we will file a complaint in the Circuit Court of Cook County, serve Mr. Voss in Arizona, and seek an order authorizing demolition with costs added to the lien, since voluntary compliance is plainly not coming. The owner has been served every notice by certified mail to the Mesa address with returns documented, so our service record is clean. Recommended next steps: (1) Board authorization for the demolition complaint and lien certification at the October 7 meeting; (2) keep the fire department's hazard notice active; (3) continue weekly re-securing until the structure comes down. Happy to draft the board memo.

subject: Direction on 1623 50th Ave - move to demolition and protect the block
body_clean: Thank you all - this is exactly the methodical work I wanted, and the case file Yolanda, Sandra, Priya, Rosa, Tom, David, and Frank have built is airtight. We are done waiting on Mr. Voss. Direction going forward: First, Yolanda, draft the board memo for the October 7 meeting requesting authorization to certify the $14,847.20 lien with the Cook County Recorder and to file the dangerous-buildings demolition complaint through Patterson & Cole, file BW-2025-117. Get it on the agenda and copy me on the draft by September 26. Second, the immediate life-safety issue overrides everything else. Rosa and Frank, I want that structure hard-secured this week - re-board the south window and rear door properly, fence the lot if needed, and coordinate directly with the fire department on the squatter and open-flame hazard Mr. Halloran reported. I do not want a fire on 50th Ave taking out the Pawlaks' garage while paperwork moves. Third, Sandra, please personally call both Mr. Halloran at 1619 and Ms. Okafor-Brown at 1620 Monroe, tell them where we are, and give them a direct number - they have carried this for six months and deserve a real human update. The fines and lien protect the taxpayers; demolition protects the block. Let us close this out the right way. Good work, everyone.
